title: Echium stachydeum
taxonomy: Echium stachydeum is a species of flowering plant in the borage family (Boraginaceae). It is native to the western Mediterranean region. Echium stachydeum is a biennial or short-lived perennial herb, with erect stems and blue or purple flowers. The plant's classification is further divided into the genus Echium and the family Boraginaceae, which is part of the larger order Borales. This classification helps to understand the plant's evolutionary relationships and its position within the plant kingdom.

Echium stachydeum's leaves and flowers may possess bioactive compounds with potential medicinal properties, although these have not been extensively studied.
